_comment: Source config for run.py. THREE capture tiers, in order of preference: (1) 'web' = headless capture.py adapters that read a site's own PUBLIC JSON/SSR data with a normal browser UA (no login/CAPTCHA/rate-limit bypass; on any 403/429/challenge we record Source Blocked and stop) — this is the cloud/CI-capable tier; (2) 'apis' = keyless/keyed JSON endpoints via capture_api.py; (3) 'ai_browser' = sources that genuinely need a visible/logged-in browser (anti-bot or login wall) and are captured by the conductor (Codex Chrome plugin / Claude-in-Chrome / Computer Use). Legacy Craigslist RSS lived under 'rss' but 403s even with a browser UA, so Craigslist now runs headless via its own sapi JSON API under 'web'.
_probe_note: Headless reachability probed 2026-06-28, rechecked 2026-06-30 and 2026-07-02 where noted (realistic desktop-Chrome UA): Craigslist sapi JSON 200; Zumper SSR __PRELOADED_STATE__ 200; Reddit JSON 403 EVERYWHERE as of 2026-07-02 (even residential — use the .rss Atom feeds in the 'rss' tier, paced 45s); Redfin ld+json 200 for Santa Clara/Sunnyvale/Mountain View but 202-challenge for San Jose/SF; PadMapper 200 but duplicates Zumper (same backend); Realtor.com 429; Roomies 403; SpareRoom 200 without clean state; Listing Project 200-but-bot-challenge; Furnished Finder 403; Zillow 403 (PerimeterX); Apartments.com 403; Facebook 400/login wall; Kopa search 404 (login-gated). Tier each source by that result.

rss.name: Reddit
rss.label: SFList
rss.note: Reddit public JSON 403s everywhere as of 2026-07-02, but .rss Atom feeds still serve (429 on back-to-back requests; run.py paces them). Lead source — most posts lack rent, so rows land in Needs Verification for manual follow-up.

_ai_browser_comment: Sources that need a visible/logged-in browser because they block headless reads (anti-bot or login wall — see _probe_note). The CONDUCTOR captures them (Codex: Chrome plugin; Claude: Claude-in-Chrome / Computer Use), writing ONE JSON array per source into the capture dir as ai-<slug>.json, then re-runs run.py. Capture VISIBLE facts only; never bypass CAPTCHA/login/rate limits; never message posters or submit anything. COVER THE WHOLE COMMUTE CORRIDOR, not just Santa Clara: South Bay (Santa Clara/Sunnyvale/Mountain View), Peninsula/Caltrain (Palo Alto, East Palo Alto, Redwood City, San Mateo, Burlingame), and SF (for the Google SF office). FB Marketplace: extract a[href*='/marketplace/item/'] cards (price/title/city); run multiple map centers via latitude/longitude/radius since one center can't reach both offices. Zillow: data is in the page's __NEXT_DATA__ (find the listResults array: zpid/detailUrl/address/price/units) — navigate per city /<city>-ca/rentals/ and read it; a real signed-in browser passes PerimeterX where headless 403s, but the exact SF 5+ filter can trigger human verification. Apartments.com: headless 403s; use the real browser and capture visible article[data-listingid] card facts from each city path.

ai_browser.name: Facebook Marketplace (corridor rentals)
ai_browser.note: Logged-in visible browser; location already follows the lat/long in the URL. Run each center in 'centers'. Scroll to lazy-load. Subleases/rooms are top priority.

ai_browser.name: Facebook Marketplace (SF 5+ bedroom rentals)
ai_browser.label: facebook-sf-5plus
ai_browser.note: Visible-browser 5+ lane. Use the SF center and search/filter text for 5 bedroom, 6 bedroom, group house, or whole house; capture only cards visibly showing 5+ bedrooms or listing text that clearly says 5+. Budget cap for the SF-only 5-person group lane is $13,250/mo total ($2,650/person); apply that cap in visible-browser filters/results.

ai_browser.name: Facebook housing groups (Bay Area sublets/rooms)
ai_browser.blocked_reason: Login wall + group membership required; noisy feed.
ai_browser.note: Groups like 'Bay Area Housing, Rooms, Sublets & Roommates'. Capture visible post facts only; do not message posters. Park if the feed is noisy/unreachable.

ai_browser.name: Zillow Rentals (corridor)
ai_browser.note: Navigate /<city>/rentals/ for each city in 'cities' and read listResults from __NEXT_DATA__ (zpid/detailUrl/address/price/units). Real signed-in browser passes PerimeterX where headless 403s.

ai_browser.name: Zillow Rentals (SF 5+ bedrooms)
ai_browser.label: zillow-sf-5plus
ai_browser.status: AI_BROWSER REQUIRED; 2026-07-01 exact 5+ URL triggered Zillow human verification
ai_browser.note: Visible-browser 5+ lane. 2026-07-01 Chrome probe: /5-_beds/ loaded but returned unfiltered SF rentals; applying the UI Beds 5+ control generated searchQueryState with beds.min=5, and direct reload of that URL showed Zillow Press & Hold human verification. Capture visible cards or __NEXT_DATA__ listResults only after the real browser loads the exact 5+ result set without challenge. Do not bypass; record Source Blocked if challenged. Budget cap for the SF-only 5-person group lane is $13,250/mo total ($2,650/person); apply that cap in visible-browser filters/results.

ai_browser.name: HotPads Rentals (SF 5+ bedrooms)
ai_browser.label: hotpads-sf-5plus
ai_browser.status: DISABLED_DUPLICATE; Zillow-owned inventory overlaps the enabled Zillow SF 5+ lane.
ai_browser.note: Visible-browser 5+ lane. Capture visible cards showing 5+ beds only. Budget cap for the SF-only 5-person group lane is $13,250/mo total ($2,650/person).

ai_browser.name: Apartments.com Rentals (corridor)
ai_browser.note: Navigate https://www.apartments.com/<city>/ for each city in 'cities'. Capture visible listing-card facts only: title/building, URL, rent/range, beds/baths, address/city, availability/fees if visible. Record Source Blocked if the visible browser hits CAPTCHA or access denial.

ai_browser.name: Apartments.com Rentals (SF 5+ bedrooms)
ai_browser.label: apartments-com-sf-5plus
ai_browser.status: AI_BROWSER REQUIRED; 2026-07-01 Chrome probe captured 9 SF 5+ cards
ai_browser.note: Visible-browser 5+ lane. Apartments.com may label this route as 4+ Beds. Scrape visible article[data-listingid] cards, parse the card's visible bed text, and keep only rows with 5+ Beds/Bedrooms/bd or listing text that clearly says 5+. Ignore 4-bedroom cards. 2026-07-01 Chrome probe on this URL found 34 cards and 9 verified SF 5+ rows. Budget cap for the SF-only 5-person group lane is $13,250/mo total ($2,650/person); apply that cap in visible-browser filters/results.

ai_browser.name: Furnished Finder (Santa Clara area)
ai_browser.blocked_reason: Old /housing/<city> route now 302s to an interactive map-search homepage; no keyless JSON results API (candidates 404 or return the SPA shell). Needs manual autocomplete selection.
ai_browser.note: Monthly furnished; flexible terms. Type the city in the search box, select the autocomplete option, then read the result cards. Park if too fiddly.

_dead_sources: Removed 2026-06-29 after browser probing: 'The Listing Project' (both thelistingproject.com and thelistingsproject.com now resolve to HugeDomains 'for sale' parking pages; the Listings Project is email/subscription-only now) and 'Kopa' (homepage shows a wind-down notice — the service is shutting down). Do not re-add unless they relaunch a public web search.
_apis_comment: Free/keyed JSON endpoints fetched by capture_api.py. No-key entries (e.g. Reddit) are best-effort; keyed entries skip until their key_env is set. {city} in url is substituted from each 'cities' value. We never add proxy/CAPTCHA/stealth code.

apis.name: Reddit
apis.note: RETIRED 2026-07-02: Reddit public JSON now 403s even from a residential IP (probed www/old/api hosts, multiple UAs). Replaced by the .rss Atom lanes in the 'rss' tier above. Keep this entry disabled unless a free Reddit OAuth token path is added.

apis.name: RentCast
apis.note: Optional, OFF by default. Legit rentals/condos API with a free tier (~50 calls/mo). Set RENTCAST_API_KEY + enabled:true to use; listings have no public URL so they land in Needs Verification.
